task_completion.description: Open the target PCAP file in Wireshark
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark GUI is open and the window title or packet list indicates that 'secure_api.pcap' has been loaded.

task_completion.description: Configure Wireshark to use the session keys log file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wireshark Preferences dialog is open on the Protocols -> TLS (or SSL) page, and the '(Pre)-Master-Secret log filename' field is set to '/home/ga/Documents/captures/session_keys.log'.
The Wireshark packet list or details pane shows decrypted HTTP traffic (e.g., HTTP GET/POST requests or HTTP/2 streams) that was previously encrypted as TLS.

task_completion.description: Find the UUID associated with the 'SECRET_FLAG:' label
task_completion.visual_evidence: The packet details pane, packet bytes pane, or a 'Follow HTTP/TLS Stream' window displays the decrypted payload containing the text 'SECRET_FLAG:' followed by a UUID string.

task_completion.description: Save only the extracted UUID to the output file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command is visible writing the extracted UUID to '/home/ga/Documents/decrypted_flag.txt'. The VLM must verify that ONLY the UUID is saved, without the 'SECRET_FLAG:' label or other extraneous text.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wireshark GUI for analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent actually interacted with the Wireshark GUI to configure the keylog and view the traffic, rather than bypassing the GUI using terminal tools like tshark.

integrity.description: The agent did not cheat by accessing the ground truth file
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there is no terminal command or file manager window showing the agent reading '/var/lib/app/ground_truth/secret_uuid.txt'.

integrity.description: The saved UUID matches the one found in the traffic
integrity.visual_evidence: The UUID being saved to 'decrypted_flag.txt' must visually match the UUID seen in the decrypted Wireshark packet payload, proving it was genuinely extracted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The PCAP file is located at /home/ga/Documents/captures/secure_api.pcap and the session keys are at /home/ga/Documents/captures/session_keys.log. The target output file is /home/ga/Documents/decrypted_flag.txt. The secret flag is a randomly generated UUID. The agent must configure Wireshark's TLS/SSL preferences to use the session keys log file to decrypt the traffic. The ground truth UUID is stored in /var/lib/app/ground_truth/secret_uuid.txt, which the agent must not access.
